fields.definition_origin: The 'tracking plan' as a discrete artifact in product analytics was named in Segment's 2018 'Analytics Academy' (segment.com), formalized further by Jeff Lash and the Iteratively/Avo schema-as-code movement (2019-2022). UXCam's 'How to Write a Tracking Plan' (uxcam.com/blog 2024) and Mixpanel's 'Top User Onboarding Metrics' (mixpanel.com 2024) codified the practice for mobile onboarding. Schema-as-code with shared TS types between client and analytics SDK descends from Iteratively (acquired by Amplitude 2021), Avo (avo.app), and Segment Protocols. Step-level granularity for onboarding became standard practice via Mobbin, Mixpanel, Amplitude case studies of high-converting funnels (Cal AI, Headspace, Headway, Mau Baron Prayer Lock).
fields.core_mechanism: A tracking plan is a single source of truth document (typically a spreadsheet, schema file, or Avo-style typed-event registry) that defines every analytics event the product emits, with strict naming convention, defined properties + types, and the user-stories the events enable. Without it, two failure modes dominate: (1) UNTRACKED MOMENTS — critical funnel transitions never instrument, leaving the team blind to where users drop off. (2) NAMING CHAOS — `step_view`, `step_seen`, `view_step`, `OnboardingStepShown` all exist in production for the same conceptual event, dashboards never align, debugging takes 10x longer. Step-level granularity (per-step events with step_id, step_index, total_steps properties) is the resolution required to identify surgical optimization targets — knowing onboarding completion is 60% is useless; knowing 22% drop on step 7 (the painpoint question, where copy is wrong) is actionable. The 2026 best-practice hybrid combines auto-capture (UXCam, Heap) for screen-by-screen exploratory analysis with explicit (PostHog, Mixpanel, Amplitude) tracking for funnel-critical moments — auto-capture catches what you forgot to instrument; explicit ensures the load-bearing events have stable schemas.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Mojo data (cited in PDF deep dive): 50% of trial starts come from the onboarding paywall — step-level visibility from welcome screen through paywall is critical to optimize the 50% revenue path. (2) Adapty 2026 onboarding analytics: top-decile teams have step-level event coverage on 100% of onboarding flow; bottom-decile have <30% coverage. (3) Mixpanel's onboarding-metrics blog (2024) cites step-completion rate as the #1 leading-indicator metric. (4) Amplitude's North-Star Framework (Amplitude 2020-2024) requires per-step instrumentation. (5) UXCam's tracking-plan blog (2024) documents 6-stage process: define, name, version, instrument, validate, evolve. (6) Iteratively / Avo customer case studies (2021-2024): teams adopting schema-as-code reduce 'analytics bug' tickets by 60-80%. (7) PostHog 2024 product report: customers using tracking-plan-as-code see 3x faster experiment iteration. (8) Piwik PRO 2024 'Track User Onboarding': practitioner walkthrough of step-level events. (9) Segment's 2024 State of Personalization Report: high-performing teams have tracking plans; low-performers don't.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Tracking plan coverage (% of funnel steps with explicit events), event schema-violation rate (events emitted with wrong/missing properties), 'analytics bug' tickets per release, time-to-funnel-debug, % of dashboards aligned to canonical event names, experiment iteration cycle time.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (no peer-reviewed RCT; broad industry practice consensus)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Indirect — tracking plan enables iteration that drives lift; standalone effect is operational (faster debugging, fewer bugs, faster experiments). Avo customer case studies cite 60-80% reduction in analytics bugs; PostHog 3x experiment velocity; cumulative conversion impact varies.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI (33-step onboarding): emits step_viewed/completed/abandoned per step plus AI plan reveal events; iterates content based on per-step abandonment heatmap. (2) Headspace 2024 onboarding: explicit funnel events visible in their public conference talks (Conf42, Mind The Product). (3) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: tracking plan visible in Starter Story playbook — emits per-step events feeding daily-iteration loop. (4) Notion's onboarding tracking plan referenced in their Notion Engineering blog. (5) BlueSky open-source codebase (github.com/bluesky-social/social-app) shows explicit onboarding events. (6) Avo's customer showcase (avo.app/customers) includes Headspace, MyFitnessPal, Patreon. (7) PostHog public dashboards (PostHog Cloud) showcase per-step funnel templates for onboarding. (8) Counter-example failure: pre-2022 Mint/Intuit onboarding had patchy events, dashboards conflicted, optimization stalled — internal practitioner reports. (9) Piwik PRO 2024 article documents event schema for onboarding step coverage. (10) Snowplow's Tracker Catalog provides reference event schemas for SaaS onboarding.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile and web share the tracking plan concept; SDK differs (PostHog RN / Mixpanel RN / Amplitude RN on mobile, web SDK on web). Mobile-specific events: app_lifecycle (foreground/background), push_permission events, ATT events, deep_link_received, app_install_attribution. Web-specific: page_viewed, ad_clicked, sign_up_form_submitted (web checkout). Cross-platform tracking plan should harmonize event names where the same conceptual moment exists (onboarding_step_viewed identical on both). Auto-capture differs: UXCam (mobile session replay), Heap (web), PostHog (both).
fields.category_fit: MANDATORY for any app investing in conversion optimization. Strongest leverage in long-onboarding categories (wellness, AI-wrapper, fintech) where step-level granularity reveals optimization targets. Acceptable for: short flows where explicit events for 4-6 key moments suffice. Skippable: hobby projects, MVPs pre-traction. Solo dev should have minimum tracking plan from Day 1 — adding analytics retroactively is technical debt.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Author plan (1-2 days), implement events (2-3 days), set up dashboards (1-2 days), validate (1 day). Solo devs benefit from PostHog (free tier generous, includes funnels + flags + experiments + session replay).
fields.tooling_options: PRIMARY: PostHog (free tier 1M events/mo, includes funnels + flags + experiments + replay), Mixpanel (free tier 100K MTU), Amplitude (free tier 10M events/mo). SCHEMA: Avo (avo.app), Iteratively (Amplitude), Segment Protocols, hand-rolled TS. AUTO-CAPTURE: UXCam, Heap, Smartlook, Hotjar (web). VALIDATION: Avo CI integration, custom GitHub Actions parsing TS event registry. WAREHOUSING: Snowplow + Snowflake / BigQuery for advanced cohort analysis. PRIVACY: Snowplow with consent integration, Mixpanel + OneTrust. NATIVE: Firebase Analytics (free, integrated GA4), AppsFlyer attribution.
fields.ai_dependency: none for the plan itself. Optional: AI-driven event-naming review (Claude for plan critique) or anomaly detection on dashboards.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to author the tracking plan AS CODE FIRST, then implement. Prompt fragment 1: 'Author the onboarding tracking plan for our 12-step flow. Output a TypeScript file `analytics/events.ts` defining a discriminated union AnalyticsEvent type with at least 20 events covering: onboarding lifecycle (started, completed, abandoned), per-step (viewed, completed, abandoned, back_navigated), personalization (token_set, token_stitched), AI plan reveal (started, completed, failed), mid-onboarding review prompt (shown, action), paywall (shown, purchased, dismissed_with_winback), push primer (shown, native_dialog_shown, opted_in, opted_out), ATT (shown, authorized, denied), resume (resumed_from_server). Each event has typed props with required fields like step_id, step_index, total_steps, step_kind, time_on_step_ms, ad_variant, locale, archetype, app_version, anonymous_id, user_id, session_id. Use snake_case verb-object naming. Implement track<E extends AnalyticsEvent>(event: E) function that compiles to PostHog/Mixpanel/Amplitude call. FORBID: untyped track() calls. FORBID: free-text user input in event properties (use sha256 hashes). Include comments documenting when_emitted and owner.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Audit the codebase for analytics calls and produce a coverage report: which onboarding screens emit step_viewed? Which dispatch step_completed on advance? Flag any screen missing instrumentation. Add CI check that fails build if events are emitted with names not in the registry.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to ad-hoc analytics calls scattered across files (`mixpanel.track('step viewed')` with inconsistent casing). AI omits step_id / step_index. AI uses spaces in event names. AI logs PII. AI doesn't add superproperties (locale, archetype, ad_variant). AI calls analytics inline in onPress handlers without abstraction. Counter-prompt: 'Define event schema FIRST as TypeScript discriminated union AnalyticsEvent. Build a single track() function in analytics/index.ts. FORBID inline mixpanel.track / posthog.capture calls in screens — must go through track(). FORBID event names with spaces — snake_case only. FORBID free-text user input as property — use sha256 hash. ALWAYS include superproperties: app_version, os, os_version, locale, anonymous_id, user_id, session_id, experiment_variants. Add CI lint that flags any string literal matching `mixpanel\.track|posthog\.capture|amplitude\.track` outside analytics/index.ts.' AI sometimes over-tracks (every tap) — counter: 'emit step-level events only at step transitions; not on every tap.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) No tracking plan — naming chaos compounds. (2) Untyped events — typo bugs at runtime. (3) PII / free-text in properties — GDPR / privacy risk. (4) Missing superproperties (locale, ad_variant) — segmentation impossible. (5) Auto-capture only — unstable for funnels (event names change as UI changes). (6) Explicit-only with no auto-capture — miss exploratory insights. (7) Event-name collisions across teams (`signup_completed` from auth team vs onboarding team). (8) High-frequency events un-batched — analytics bill explodes. (9) Schema changes without versioning — historical dashboards break. (10) Tracking plan written but never enforced — dev drift. (11) No owner per event — orphaned events accumulate. (12) Mid-flow analytics fail silently — events lost. (13) Same event firing twice from different code paths — funnel double-counts.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (a) GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) data minimization — emit only properties tied to declared analytics purpose. (b) GDPR Art. 13 transparency — declare in privacy notice. (c) GDPR consent (Art. 6 / 7): EU users must consent to analytics tracking; integrate CMP (item 47). (d) CCPA opt-out for sale of data — declare data-sharing with analytics vendors. (e) COPPA 2026 — under-13s require verifiable parental consent; default-off analytics for minors. (f) PII minimization — never log free-text user answers; use hashes. (g) iOS ATT — analytics that use IDFA require ATT opt-in; PostHog/Mixpanel default to non-IDFA. (h) Apple App Privacy Report and Privacy Nutrition Label must accurately reflect tracked properties. (i) DFA Q4 2026 — manipulative-pattern detection may scrutinize analytics-driven dark patterns; maintain audit trail of why each event is collected.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.2 data use and sharing — declare in Privacy Nutrition Label. Apple PrivacyManifest (mandatory 2024+) — analytics SDKs must declare reasons; PostHog/Mixpanel/Amplitude have compliant manifests. Google Play Data Safety form — declare. iOS ATT — opt-in flow required for IDFA tracking (most analytics vendors default to non-IDFA). Apple Guideline 4.5.2 — third-party SDK aggregation prohibited unless compliant.
fields.accessibility_implications: N/A direct. Indirect: tracking plan can include accessibility-related events (screen_reader_active_session, dynamic_type_size, prefers_reduced_motion) to measure a11y user experience and inform design decisions.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Tracking plan ages — review quarterly. (2) Analytics SDK upgrades can break event semantics — pin versions, test before upgrade. (3) Event volume cost — Mixpanel/Amplitude bill on MTU + events; budget. (4) Sampling — high-frequency events sampled, low-frequency (onboarding) not. (5) Off-line event queue — analytics SDKs handle offline differently; verify behavior. (6) GDPR consent before tracking — events should not fire pre-consent (or fire with consent=false flag and server filters). (7) Dev/staging/prod environments — separate projects to avoid pollution. (8) Time zone in timestamps — server-side ISO UTC always. (9) Event property explosion — limit to 25-50 per event; high-cardinality properties (free-text) ruin warehouse performance. (10) Funnel definition stability — once dashboards rely on event sequence, changing semantics breaks history.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: N/A operational. Tracking plan is locale-agnostic; locale itself is a property. Privacy regulations vary by region (handle in CMP, item 47).
fields.prescribed_action: Author the tracking plan as TypeScript discriminated union AnalyticsEvent before writing analytics code. Define minimum 20 onboarding events with strict properties (step_id, step_index, total_steps, step_kind, time_on_step_ms, ad_variant, locale, archetype, plus superproperties). Use snake_case verb-object naming. Implement single track<E extends AnalyticsEvent>() function; forbid inline analytics calls. Hash user-input values, never log free text. Pair explicit tracking with auto-capture (UXCam) for exploratory coverage. Validate via CI. Forbid: ad-hoc analytics calls, untyped events, PII in properties, missing superproperties.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — every questionnaire step emits step_viewed/completed/abandoned with step_kind property feeding question-type-mix optimization (item 27). Per-step abandonment heatmap drives copy iteration.
fields.aha_relevance: Direct — measure ai_plan_reveal_started/completed/failed to instrument the engineered Aha (items 12, 13). Latency, completion, and downstream paywall_shown rates measure Aha effectiveness.
fields.telemetry_events: (self-defining — see canonical event list in engineering_tactics)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: N/A operational — though archetype is a recommended superproperty for archetype-conditioned analysis.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'analytics naming chaos' and 'untracked critical moments'. Pairs with item 44 (Funnel Analysis + Cohort/LTV) — events feed funnels. Pairs with item 45 (Feature Flag + A/B Test) — experiment_variants is a superproperty. Pairs with item 31 (SDUI) — config_version is a superproperty.
uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion lift attributable to tracking plan adoption (operational benefit; effect indirect via faster iteration)
